message:
perf(core): cache concurrent handle to bypass per-call registry mutex (bd-ifgl7)

Eliminates a hot-path mutex acquisition inside the concurrent
transaction VDBE setup by caching the SharedConcurrentHandle
lookup on the `Connection` for the duration of a session.

Previously, `setup_concurrent_exec_context` called
`lock_unpoisoned(&self.concurrent_registry).handle(session_id)` on
every VDBE step that constructs a ConcurrentExecContext. Under a
multi-agent concurrent workload that can tap the VDBE engine very
frequently, the `concurrent_registry` mutex was a measurable
contention point even though the handle it returns is stable for
the entire lifetime of a concurrent transaction.

Changes:

- Add `cached_concurrent_handle: RefCell<Option<(u64,
  SharedConcurrentHandle)>>` field on `Connection`. The tuple
  binds the cached handle to the session_id it belongs to so a
  stale cache from a prior transaction cannot be reused after a
  new BEGIN.

- Initialize `cached_concurrent_handle` to `None` in both
  Connection constructors (the primary open path at ~L6741 and
  the reopen/clone path at ~L7017).

- In `setup_concurrent_exec_context`, replace the unconditional
  registry lookup with a two-step check:
    1. Read-borrow `cached_concurrent_handle`. If the cached
       session_id matches the current one, Arc::clone the cached
       handle (zero lock contention).
    2. Otherwise, drop the borrow, perform the registry lookup
       once, populate the cache via `borrow_mut`, and return the
       freshly cached handle.

The cache key is `(session_id, handle)` — when a new concurrent
session begins with a different id the else-branch automatically
refreshes the cache, so no explicit invalidation is required on
COMMIT/ROLLBACK. The cache lives on `RefCell` because
`Connection` is not Send (already the case for the surrounding
interior-mutability pattern used for `concurrent_session_id`).

The "`concurrent transaction missing handle during VDBE setup`"
error path is preserved in both branches so a corrupted registry
state still surfaces as `FrankenError::Internal` rather than
silently falling back to the cache.

This sits on top of the 24 already-committed perf/test/feature
commits in this branch (hash-join permutation, MVCC/EBR retire
fast-path, btree cell-slot cache, Vec pre-sizing audit, morsel
INSERT dispatch, etc.) and continues the Wave 5 concurrent-writer
profile work (bd-m4s2c).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
